about maths stuff atom feed

Pandoc latex2markdown test

by Tim Holzschuh on January 12, 2021

Tagged as: pandoc, latex.

For this chapter, we fix a category \(\mathcal{C}\) satisfying

  1. \(\mathcal{C}\) admits finite limits

  2. \(\mathcal{C}\) admits finite coproducts and quotients by finite groups of automorphisms

  3. Every morphism \(X \xrightarrow{u} Y\) of \(\mathcal{C}\) factorizes as \(u = u''\circ u'\) with \(u'\) an and \(u''\) a and every monomorphism \(X \xrightarrow{u} Y\) identifies \(X\) with a direct summand of \(Y\).

For a choice of such a category \(\mathcal{C}\) we further fix a functor \(F \colon \mathcal{C} \to \textit{fsets}\) satisfying

  1. \(F\) is left exact

  2. \(F\) commutes with coproducts, quotients by finite groups of automorphisms and carries epimorphisms into epimorphisms

  3. \(F\) is conservative, i.e. \(u\) is an isomorphism if \(Fu\) is

In the following, we will give an alternative characterization of categories \(\mathcal{C}\) satisfying \((G1)\)-\((G3)\) and admitting a functor \(F\) satisfying \((G4)\)-\((G6)\) resulting in the notion of \(\textit{Galois Category}\)
